paragraphs.body: Often people walk past important signs or cues in their life. More often they walk past meaningless pieces of junk that they don’t think twice about. But if you try your hardest to clear your preconceptions and ignorance, sometimes these categories can overlap. There is no rhyme or reason that makes something special for any given person. That depends on the kind of person they are. You cannot apply an objective criterion for what something means to someone. If there was one thing that everyone could try to improve at, it is to appreciate things that other people appreciate, even if you don’t understand it.

I have lived in Pittsburgh all my life. I have been coming to school in Oakland for more than 4 years. Apart from 9th grade, I attended virtually in my bedroom as everyone else did during 2020-2021. All my high school years and now my current university years I have spent on 5th avenue. It wasn’t until the week of August 18th that I moved into Nordenberg Hall on 5th Avenue and University Place that I spotted it. Just a quick walk across the street on the opposite corner of the Nordenberg entrance, was a storm drain that smelled particularly gross. This isn’t the strangest part of the road thought, because wedged into the corner of the storm drain and the concrete was an old, battered baseball.

annotations.text: Just a quick walk across the street on the opposite corner of the Nordenberg entrance, was a storm drain that smelled particularly gross.

paragraphs.body: The bright red stitching is already starting to come undone, indicating how long it may have been rotting down there. The standard sharp white façade had long been faded. It was more of a mixture of beige, grey and white. Spotted with specks of dirt and whatever other grime finds its way to the storm drain. If I ever dared to touch it, I would assume the sleek, smooth texture would be gone. Replaced by rough edges and a bumpy surface. Judging by how tight the ball is wedged into the road, the ball has lost its circular shape and warped into something else. I imagine that even after all this time, if someone were to pry it out of the ground and decided to play some catch, that the classic whoosh and crunch of throwing the ball into the glove would sound the same. If someone were to lose a bet and be forced to stoop down low and have a taste of the grimy ball, it wouldn’t be pleasant. It would taste of dirt, dust, rotten food, empty wrappers, and whatever other trash finds its way into the storm drain. If someone were to have a taste, they would also get a whiff of the most putrid smell ever. Standing up next to the storm drain, you can smell the rotten waste. Even after you cross the street, the smell follows you, trailing you until you walk at least 15-20 feet away.

annotations.comment: Nice job with descriptive writing! This is a good move because it asks your reader to focus very intently on your object, thus rendering it important for the essay.
annotations.context_left: you cross the street, the smell follows you, trailing you until you walk at least 15-20 feet away.

paragraphs.body: I used to play baseball as a kid. Although to be completely honest, I never really enjoyed the sport. I never watched baseball on TV, I rarely went to see the Pirates play, and I preferred to play soccer or football. Despite this, I started playing on a little league team when I was 12 or 13. My best friend always loved to play baseball and had played the sport as soon as he was old enough to hold a bat and through a ball. I joined the Arizona Diamondbacks. It took me some time to adapt, but I eventually found a spot playing in outfield or sometimes even at catcher. We won the little league championship the year I joined. The year after that I quit. This was the start of me trying something else in my life. I was still young, but I wanted to do the things that brought meaning and value to me. Despite winning it all, I never felt a connection to then sport.

annotations.text: I used to play baseball as a kid. Although to be completely honest, I never really enjoyed the sport. I never watched baseball on TV, I rarely went to see the Pirates play,
annotations.comment: great voice and transition! Excellent move.
This is an engaging paragraph. Nicely done.
annotations.context_left: t brought meaning and value to me. Despite winning it all, I never felt a connection to then sport.

paragraphs.body: Back during elementary school, my family and I went on a mission trip to Haiti with a church group. While we were there, we took one vacation day apart and went to visit the massive fortress up in the mountainside, The Citadel. Our trip started with us taking a long and winding rocky road up the mountain, on the backs of donkeys. The smell was putrid, and the heat was unbearable. Wearing sunglasses and a hat was almost mandatory, as well as loading up on water. I don’t remember how many times I had to reapply my sunscreen. The sun beating down on us all day, making us sweat and draining our energy. After what seemed like an eternity on that steep narrow road to the top, we made it. Even though we didn’t walk to the top, it felt like we had, judging by how tired we were. What made it all worth it was the immense beauty and wonder of the fortress. Standing at the top, the sun gleaming in the sky revealing the beautiful landscape for miles and miles on end.

annotations.text: Our trip started with us taking a long and winding rocky road up the mountain, on the backs of donkeys.

annotations.comment: The details throughout this paragraph are beautiful. I wonder, though: what is the revelation of this narrative? What "big idea" does it create?
annotations.context_left: the top, the sun gleaming in the sky revealing the beautiful landscape for miles and miles on end.

paragraphs.body: The sun is a star in the sky, but the sun is not just a glowing ball of light. It is not just a nearly perfect sphere of plasma in the sky. The sun does not necessarily represent all that is good and right. The sun doesn’t favor a certain culture or society. What the sun does is provide us with light to work, talk, walk, and play. The sun does wake us up and tells us to start our day. The sun winds us down and tells us to go to sleep. The sun lets us follow our daily desires. It fuels our dreams and aspirations. It can give us light to see the path clearly ahead. The sun can also, sear our skin and burn our eyes. It can wither our crops and burn our fields. It can reveal the ugly and dirty things hidden in the dark. The sun doesn’t pick friends or foes, it just casts a steady and clear message, day after day. The sun is reliable and unbiased. The sun can reveal the lost and buried treasures around us. It can reveal the hidden truths about us and sets a clear picture for our day. The sun is ever steady in lighting up the path, no matter if the path is thorny and dangerous, or beautiful and clear.

annotations.comment: I also wonder how this definition relates to your object? How are the sun and the baseball synthesizing together for the purpose of this essay? What _is_ your essay's central focusing idea?
annotations.context_left: ady in lighting up the path, no matter if the path is thorny and dangerous, or beautiful and clear.

paragraphs.body: What the baseball trapped in the storm drain means to me is something I’m not sure of. But what I am sure of is that every day when I pass by, I look down to make sure that it’s still there. It holds significance to me, even if it’s meaningless for most people. When I move out of Nordenberg Hall and go back home, I will still wonder if the baseball is holding onto life. I also appreciate the sun very much, but for different reasons from the baseball. The sun provides everyone with something tangible. Light, energy, sometimes brutal sunburns and sweltering rays of heat. But it has something to offer to everyone. The baseball offers something unique to me. Everyone has unique feelings, thoughts and ideas that mean something more than to them than to someone else. It’s worth searching far and wide to understand why someone places value on something, which can lead to better understanding of each other and ourselves.

annotations.text: What the baseball trapped in the storm drain means to me is something I’m not sure of. But what I am sure of is that every day when I pass by, I look down to make sure that it’s still there.
annotations.comment: Aha. Well this is definitely something for us to discuss then. :) Remember that the odd object essay works because the object becomes a metaphor. So the essay, and the essay's author, needs to know what that metaphor is.

So many great ideas emerging in this essay--excellent details and a strong voice that's engaging and fun to read. I'm sure there's apowerful metaphor bubbling right beneath the surface, too. Let's go find it! On that note: who is this essay's audience? Purpose? Let's talk about your essay's "map" so that we can figure out how you might leverage your definition paragraph to clearly illuminate youressay's central focusing idea. Is "sun" the _right_ word? Let's talk. Well done!
